# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
# Reference geometry for kshana lunar navigation service volume (module lunar_service).
# Oracle: ANISE 0.10.2 astro::Orbit Keplerian two-body propagator
#         (try_keplerian_mean_anomaly + at_epoch, equinoctial two-body),
#         Christopher Rabotin / Nyx Space Foundation, MPL-2.0.
#         An INDEPENDENT Kepler solver (GMAT-derived element conversion +
#         equinoctial mean-longitude advance) vs kshana's Newton-Raphson Kepler.
# Generated by xval/anise-service-geometry (workspace-excluded; own Cargo.lock).
#   Reproduce: cargo run --release  (in that crate)
# Constellation (kshana default scenario template): 8 sats,
#   sma_km=9737.4, ecc=0.6, inc_deg=57.7, argp_deg=90,
#   raan_deg = mean_anom_deg = 360*k/8 for k=0..8.
# Moon GM (km^3/s^2) attached to the ANISE frame: 4902.800118000
#   (= kshana MOON_GM_M3_S2 = 4902800118000 m^3/s^2 / 1e9).
# R_MOON_M = 1737400; elevation mask = 5 deg.
# Grid: 7 lat (-90..-30 step 10) x 6 lon (-180..120 step 60) = 42 pts;
#   12 epochs (0..39600 s, 3600 s step).
# Row formats:
#   MCI <ei> <si> <t_s> <x_m> <y_m> <z_m>   (ANISE MCI position of sat si at epoch ei)
#   VIS <ei> <gi> <t_s> <lat_deg> <lon_deg> <n_visible> <sat_indices|->
#     (ANISE-derived visible-satellite SET at grid point gi, epoch ei, above mask)
# Honest scope: validates the PROPAGATION + VISIBILITY GEOMETRY only. The DOP
#   kernel (vs gnss_lib_py) and the LunaNet LNIS ARAIM PL budget are validated /
#   modelled elsewhere; the constellation is illustrative public-source, not the
#   real Moonlight/LCNS ephemeris.
